title: Kind of Blue
label: Columbia
description: Every significant pressing of Miles Davis's Kind of Blue — the speed error, tape provenance, and a full reissue hierarchy from original Six Eye to AP UHQR.

title: A Love Supreme
label: Impulse!
description: Pressing guide for A Love Supreme — the Abbey Road safety tape, mono vs stereo, and every reissue from original orange Impulse! to the AP UHQR.

title: Time Out
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Dave Brubeck Quartet's Time Out — Columbia Six Eye identification, Classic Records and Analogue Productions reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: The Shape of Jazz to Come
label: Atlantic
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Ornette Coleman's The Shape of Jazz to Come — Atlantic bullseye label identification, ORG, Speakers Corner and VMP reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Giant Steps
label: Atlantic
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for John Coltrane's Giant Steps — Atlantic black label identification, mono vs. stereo analysis, AP Atlantic 75 and Rhino 60th Anniversary reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Mingus Ah Um
label: Columbia
description: Pressing guide for Mingus Ah Um — the edit problem, Six Eye identification, and every pressing from original Columbia to the ORG 45rpm and MoFi One-Step.

title: Bitches Brew
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Miles Davis's Bitches Brew — Columbia 360 Sound two-eye label identification, Mobile Fidelity and One-Step reissue hierarchy, tape provenance, and buyer's guide.

title: Getz/Gilberto
label: Verve
description: Pressing guide for Getz/Gilberto — the channel reversal problem, Phil Ramone's original master tape, and every pressing from 1964 Verve to the Impex 1Step.

title: Moanin'
artist: Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Moanin' — Blue Note original identification, the A vs A-1 matrix debate, and every pressing from original Van Gelder to Music Matters SRX.

title: Saxophone Colossus
label: Prestige
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Sonny Rollins's Saxophone Colossus — Prestige yellow label identification, RVG stamp authentication, Analogue Productions 200g and European pressing hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Head Hunters
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Herbie Hancock's Head Hunters — Columbia KC 32731 original pressing identification, Analogue Productions 45rpm and 33rpm reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Speak No Evil
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Speak No Evil — Van Gelder's Englewood Cliffs peak, native mono and stereo tapes, and every reissue from original New York USA label to Music Matters AAA.

title: Out to Lunch!
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Out to Lunch! — Dolphy's final album as leader, mono strongly preferred, and every reissue from original Blue Note to Tone Poet.

title: The Köln Concert
label: ECM
description: Pressing guide for The Köln Concert — the near-cancelled solo piano session, German label variants, and the 2010 Pallas 180g as the modern reference.

title: Somethin' Else
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Somethin' Else — the final Hackensack session with Miles Davis on loan, mono only, and every reissue from original Blue Note 47 West 63rd to the MoFi One-Step.

title: Song for My Father
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Song for My Father — three Van Gelder sessions, two quintets, the bossa nova inspiration, and the Music Matters 45rpm as the only pressing to fix the title track's tape warble.

title: Maiden Voyage
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Maiden Voyage — native mono and stereo tapes, Tony Williams's drums in stereo, and every reissue from original New York USA label to Music Matters AAA.

title: The Black Saint and the Sinner Lady
label: Impulse!
description: Pressing guide for The Black Saint and the Sinner Lady — first jazz album to use overdubbing, Bob Simpson engineering, and every reissue from original orange Impulse! to the AP 45rpm and Acoustic Sounds series.

title: The Sidewinder
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for The Sidewinder — Blue Note's best-selling record ever, the boogaloo groove, and every reissue from original New York USA label to Music Matters 45rpm.

title: Blue Train
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Blue Train — Coltrane's only Blue Note leadership session, Hackensack living room acoustics, the NY23 label chronology, and every reissue from original to Tone Poet mono.

title: Birth of the Cool
label: Capitol
description: Pressing guide for Birth of the Cool — three Capitol sessions 1949–50, mono only (no stereo was ever made), turquoise label variants, and the Tone Poet 2026 as the definitive modern pressing.

title: Sunday at the Village Vanguard
label: Riverside
description: Pressing guide for Sunday at the Village Vanguard — single 2-track session, mono vs stereo, and every reissue from original Riverside to the AP UHQR.

title: My Favorite Things
label: Atlantic
description: Pressing guide for My Favorite Things — the lost and found mono master, Atlantic label hierarchy, and every pressing from original white fan to the AP Atlantic 75 series.

title: Brilliant Corners
label: Riverside
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Brilliant Corners by Thelonious Monk – the mono-only recording, the fake-stereo RLP 1174 curiosity, and a full reissue hierarchy from the original Riverside pressing to ERC.

title: Sketches of Spain
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Sketches of Spain by Miles Davis – six-eye stereo identification, the Classic Records vs. Mobile Fidelity mastering lineages, and a full reissue hierarchy.

title: Heavy Weather
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Heavy Weather by Weather Report.

title: The Blues and the Abstract Truth
label: Impulse!
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Blues and the Abstract Truth by Oliver Nelson.

title: Soul Station
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Soul Station by Hank Mobley.

title: In a Silent Way
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for In a Silent Way by Miles Davis.

title: Go!
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Go! by Dexter Gordon.

title: The Incredible Jazz Guitar of Wes Montgomery
label: Riverside
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Incredible Jazz Guitar of Wes Montgomery – the lost master tape, the mother-dub reissue lineage, and the 2025 Craft Recordings tape-sourced remaster.

title: Clifford Brown & Max Roach
label: EmArcy
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Clifford Brown & Max Roach by Clifford Brown & Max Roach.

title: The Inner Mounting Flame
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Inner Mounting Flame by Mahavishnu Orchestra.

title: Karma
label: Impulse!
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Karma by Pharoah Sanders – Impulse! label-variant dating and the 2022 Verve Acoustic Sounds Series reissue.

title: Milestones
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Milestones by Miles Davis – the mono-only recording, the 1967 fake-stereo release vs. the legitimate 2000 Mark Wilder stereo remix, and the Sony Japan reissue as the modern reference.

title: Ellington at Newport
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Ellington at Newport by Duke Ellington.

title: Idle Moments
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Idle Moments by Grant Green – the accidental 15-minute title track, and the full reissue hierarchy from original Blue Note to Analogue Productions and Music Matters 45rpm.

title: Genius of Modern Music Volume 1
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Genius of Modern Music Volume 1 – Monk's first Blue Note sessions, the 10" vs 12" reissue history, and why source-recording limitations, not pressing quality, set the sonic ceiling.

title: The Real McCoy
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Real McCoy by McCoy Tyner – Tyner's first Blue Note leader date, the Blue Note/Liberty label transition, and why the genuine first pressing already carries Liberty labels.

title: Waltz for Debby
label: Riverside
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Waltz for Debby by the Bill Evans Trio – the Village Vanguard session, Scott LaFaro's final recording, and a four-decade reissue rivalry between Doug Sax, Kevin Gray, and Steve Hoffman.

title: The Epic
label: Brainfeeder
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Epic by Kamasi Washington – why every vinyl pressing of this 172-minute triple LP shares the same side-length compromise, and why CD/digital is often the better choice for critical listening.

title: Point of Departure
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Point of Departure by Andrew Hill – Eric Dolphy's final American studio session, and a clean reissue hierarchy from the original Blue Note pressing to the Music Matters 45rpm.

title: Ella and Louis
label: Verve
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Ella and Louis – the single-day session backed by the Oscar Peterson Quartet, mono only despite the three-track tape, and the Analogue Productions 45rpm as the reference edition.

title: Return to Forever
label: ECM
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Return to Forever by Chick Corea – the debut of the band that took its name from this album, held back from US release for three years, and documented noise issues on both the German and US originals.

title: Journey in Satchidananda
label: Impulse!
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Journey in Satchidananda by Alice Coltrane – recorded three years after John Coltrane's death, and widely regarded as her masterpiece and a foundational spiritual jazz document.

title: Complete Savoy and Dial Studio Recordings
label: Savoy/Dial
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Complete Savoy and Dial Studio Recordings – Charlie Parker's foundational bebop sessions across two labels, compiled by Orrin Keepnews, and the debated merits of the 2015 10-LP vinyl box against earlier reissue lineages.

title: Night Train
label: Verve
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Night Train by the Oscar Peterson Trio – the trio's best-selling album, and the session where "Hymn to Freedom" was written on the spot and later became a Civil Rights Movement anthem.

title: Monk's Dream
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Monk's Dream by Thelonious Monk – his best-selling album and Columbia debut, and the Impex Records reissue that's become the modern standard.

title: Concert by the Sea
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Concert by the Sea by Erroll Garner – recorded almost by accident for a single Armed Forces radio broadcast, and one of the best-selling jazz albums in history.

title: Empyrean Isles
label: Blue Note
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Empyrean Isles by Herbie Hancock – the first Hancock/Carter/Williams session without Miles Davis, featuring the debut of "Cantaloupe Island," and the reissue hierarchy from original Blue Note to Music Matters.
